name: materialize_definitions
title: Materialize Definitions
description: Declared generation jobs (plan/vocabulary/61-materialize-spec.md): one record per definition -- a source collection, an optional flat-equality filter, a trigger (scheduled/scheduled_fixed/event), an output collection (optionally with children), an idempotency_key template, and a mapping. The daemon's materialize pass (object_daemon.process_materializations, object_materialize.generate_config) generates due output; materialize_run (a slot button on this collection's own detail page) and materialize_seed (an event handler for event-mode definitions) call the identical per-source-row generation function. filter/trigger/mapping/balance_check are stored as JSON text -- this schema does not parse or validate their structure; object_materialize.parse_definition does that at generation time, same convention rollup_definitions uses for its own JSON-shaped fields. Admin-owned, full stop (see permissions/rules.json): a definition's mapping/trigger shape can generate financial records. debit_account_id/credit_account_id are this schema's own resolution of 61's Open Question on where depreciation's two posting accounts live -- on the definition record itself (fewest moving parts), only meaningful for the synthesized depreciation-lines shape (a child_collection with no child_source_field).

fields.name: source_collection
fields.label: Source Collection
fields.help: Exactly one collection -- no joins. The materialize pass reads it outside row-level permissions (daemon posture), so it must already exist.

fields.name: source_filter
fields.label: Source Filter (JSON)
fields.help: Optional flat {field: value} object, ANDed on equality -- e.g. {"is_active": true}. Blank means every row. Same flat-equality-only language as rollup_definitions.filter.

fields.name: trigger
fields.label: Trigger (JSON)
fields.help: Exactly one of three shapes: {"mode": "scheduled", "interval_seconds": N, "period_field": <source row field naming its own frequency, e.g. "frequency">, "anchor_field": <source row date field>} for a per-row-frequency recurring job; {"mode": "scheduled_fixed", "interval_seconds": N, "period_field": <a FIXED granularity, e.g. "monthly">, "start_field": <source row date field>, "end_condition": {"periods_field": <source row integer field capping total periods>}} for a depreciation-shaped job; or {"mode": "event", "on": "record.created"} for CreateWork (requires DBBASIC_ENABLE_EVENT_HANDLERS; degrades to manual-only when unset). Manual run-now is always available regardless of mode.

fields.name: output_collection
fields.label: Output Collection
fields.help: Where the generated top-level record lands -- an EXISTING, already-schema'd collection this package does not own. May equal source_collection (CreateWork: an in-place update, not a create).

fields.name: child_collection
fields.label: Child Collection
fields.help: Optional. Present only when the generated record has child rows (append-only postings, e.g. fin_journal_lines). Absent for a plain single-record generation (e.g. CreateWork).

fields.name: child_source_field
fields.label: Child Source Field
fields.help: Names the field on the SOURCE row holding the child line template data as a JSON list (e.g. fin_recurring.template_lines), parsed verbatim into child rows. Leave blank alongside a set child_collection for the synthesized depreciation-lines shape instead (see debit_account_id/credit_account_id below).

fields.name: child_link_field
fields.label: Child Link Field
fields.help: Names the FK field on each generated child row back to the generated parent (e.g. fin_journal_lines.journal_id). Required whenever child_collection is set.

fields.name: idempotency_key
fields.label: Idempotency Key Template
fields.help: A template producing the DETERMINISTIC id of the generated top-level record: {definition_id}, {source_id}, and (scheduled/scheduled_fixed only) {period_start}, e.g. "matgen_{definition_id}_{source_id}_{period_start}". This key is the entire idempotency mechanism -- treat as effectively immutable once a definition has generated at least one record (operational guidance, not enforced, same posture as rollup_definitions' target_collection).

fields.name: mapping
fields.label: Mapping (JSON)
fields.help: One entry per output field, each a small closed vocabulary: {"from": <source field>}, {"from_period": "period_start"|"period_end"|"period_label"}, {"literal": <value>}, {"template": "...{source.field}...{period_label}..."}, {"if": {"source_field":..., "equals":...}, "then":..., "else":...}, or the depreciation-specific {"depreciation_amount": {"method": "straight_line", "cost_field":..., "salvage_field":..., "life_field":...}} (declining is rejected, not silently mis-computed). The synthesized depreciation-lines shape reserves the key "amount" for this last op.

fields.name: balance_check
fields.label: Balance Check (JSON)
fields.help: Optional {"debit_field", "credit_field"} naming the child rows' amount fields. When present, generated children's debit/credit sums (exact integer cents) MUST be equal before ANY write happens -- zero rows land otherwise. Required for the synthesized depreciation-lines shape.

fields.name: debit_account_id
fields.label: Debit Account (depreciation)
fields.help: Only meaningful for the synthesized depreciation-lines shape (child_collection set, child_source_field blank): the account debited each period (typically Depreciation Expense).

fields.name: credit_account_id
fields.label: Credit Account (depreciation)
fields.help: Only meaningful for the synthesized depreciation-lines shape: the account credited each period (typically Accumulated Depreciation).

fields.name: actor
fields.label: Actor
fields.help: Attribution string stamped on every write this definition's generations make. Defaults to "daemon:materialize" when blank; override for finer-grained audit filtering, e.g. "daemon:materialize:depreciation".

fields.name: last_run_at
fields.label: Last Run
fields.help: Server-set after a pass over this definition (whether or not it generated anything that pass). Freshness DISPLAY only -- the actual due-set is recomputed from first principles every pass, never trusted from this field.

fields.name: enabled
fields.label: Enabled
fields.help: Per-definition pause, independent of the instance-wide materialize_enabled flag. Resume any time without further thought.

fields.name: block
fields.label: Blocked
fields.help: The harder stop: refuses even materialize_run's manual path with a distinct error. For a definition an operator has identified as producing genuinely wrong output -- "something is wrong here, don't run this even on purpose," unlike enabled: false's "pause, resume later."
